description: Production monitoring for Parakeet RNNT 1.1B ASR service on GKE (L4 GPU). Covers SRE golden signals, ASR-specific quality metrics (RTFx, audio/inference/queue latency distributions), GPU health via DCGM, and pod lifecycle.

links.title: GKE Pod Resources
links.tooltip: Kubernetes compute resources for parakeet pod

links.title: Node Exporter
links.tooltip: Node-level metrics for parakeet-pool GPU nodes

panels.description: Percentage of requests returning success status. Target: >99.9%.
panels.title: Success Rate

panels.description: 95th percentile request latency. Measures worst-case user-facing delay.
panels.title: p95 Latency

panels.description: Number of active WebSocket streaming connections to the ASR service.
panels.title: Active Streams

panels.description: Current GPU compute utilization from DCGM. Sustained >95% may indicate saturation.
panels.title: GPU Utilization %

panels.description: RTFx > 1.0 means faster than real-time. Higher is better.
panels.title: Real-Time Factor (RTFx)

panels.description: Real-time factor trend. >1 = faster than real-time.
panels.title: RTFx Over Time

panels.description: Requests/sec broken down by status (success/error/oom).
panels.title: Request Rate by Status

panels.description: Total GPU out-of-memory events. Alert if > 0.
panels.title: GPU OOM Events

panels.description: Time requests spend waiting in the batch queue.
panels.title: Queue Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: GPU inference time per batch.
panels.title: Inference Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Duration of audio segments being processed.
panels.title: Audio Duration Distribution (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Total hours of audio transcribed in the selected dashboard time range.
panels.title: Audio Transcribed (range)

panels.description: Hours of audio transcribed in each 1-hour interval. Shows transcription workload over time.
panels.title: Audio Volume Per Hour

panels.description: Requests per second across all endpoints. Baseline ~7 RPS at peak traffic.
panels.title: Request Rate (RPS)

panels.description: Request latency at p50, p95, and p99 percentiles. p99 spikes indicate tail latency issues.
panels.title: Latency Percentiles

panels.description: Percentage of requests returning errors. Sustained >1% warrants investigation.
panels.title: Error Rate %

panels.description: Latency distribution heatmap showing request density across time and duration buckets.
panels.title: Latency Heatmap

panels.description: Requests waiting in the batch queue. High values indicate GPU can't keep up with demand.
panels.title: Pending Requests (Queue)

panels.description: Requests currently being processed in a GPU batch. Bound by max batch size (64).
panels.title: Active Batch Requests

panels.description: Active streaming connections over time. Correlates with concurrent users.
panels.title: Active Streams

panels.description: Average batch size per inference call. Higher = better GPU utilization. Max 64.
panels.title: Batch Size Distribution (avg)

panels.description: GPU compute core utilization percentage from DCGM exporter. Filtered to parakeet-pool nodes.
panels.title: GPU Utilization %

panels.description: GPU VRAM usage vs total available (22.5 GB on L4). OOM risk above ~90%.
panels.title: VRAM Used / Free

panels.description: GPU die temperature in Celsius. L4 throttles at 97°C. Sustained >85°C is a warning.
panels.title: GPU Temperature

panels.description: GPU power draw in watts. L4 TDP is 72W. Power throttling is the #1 RTFx degradation cause.
panels.title: Power Usage (W)

panels.description: GPU streaming multiprocessor clock speed. Drops below base clock indicate thermal/power throttling.
panels.title: SM Clock (MHz)

panels.description: Tensor core pipe utilization. Shows how effectively the model uses GPU tensor cores for inference.
panels.title: Tensor Core Utilization

panels.description: Pod CPU usage vs configured limit. Sustained near-limit indicates CPU contention risk.
panels.title: Pod CPU vs Limit

panels.description: Pod memory usage vs configured limit. Approaching limit triggers OOMKill.
panels.title: Pod Memory vs Limit

panels.description: Container restart count in the last hour. Non-zero indicates instability (crash loops, OOM, liveness failures).
panels.title: Container Restarts (1h)

panels.description: OOMKill events from Kubernetes. Any occurrence is critical — indicates memory limit too low or memory leak.
panels.title: OOM Kill Events

panels.description: PCIe bus bandwidth utilization for data transfer between CPU and GPU. Bottleneck if sustained >80%.
panels.title: PCIe Bandwidth TX/RX

panels.description: GPU DRAM (VRAM) memory bandwidth utilization. High values indicate memory-bound workloads.
panels.title: DRAM Bandwidth Utilization

panels.description: Percentage of streaming multiprocessors actively executing work. Low SM active with high GPU util suggests memory stalls.
panels.title: SM Active

panels.description: Shows HPA scaling decisions. Gap between desired and current indicates scaling lag. Sustained max replicas may signal capacity exhaustion.
panels.title: HPA Replicas (Ready / Total / Desired)

panels.description: Requests hitting the internal load balancer for parakeet. Compare with server-side Request Rate by Status to detect dropped requests — if LB shows requests that the server doesn't, they were lost before reaching the pod.
panels.title: LB Request Rate by Response Code

panels.description: Latency as measured by the internal load balancer. Includes queue time + network + server processing. Spikes here during deploys indicate requests waiting for pods to become ready.
panels.title: LB Backend Latency (p50/p95)

panels.description: Compares LB-level request rate with server-side rate. If the LB line is higher than the Server line, requests are being dropped before reaching the pod — typically during deploys, OOM kills, or pod restarts.
panels.title: LB vs Server Request Rate (Drop Detection)

title: Parakeet ASR Monitoring
